paragraphs.body: Is This Property Right for You?

In the video, “Buying your first rental property,” John Liang analyzes the benefits of investing in real estate. He expresses his point by developing several important ideas. First, Liang explains the tax advantages involved in owning properties. Each year your property will typically increase in value in terms of how much you could sell the property for. However, in the eyes of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), they see a depreciation in the property since it is getting older. This results in the ability to write off the depreciated amount of your property deemed by the IRS in your taxes. Next, he introduces the benefits of equity. As your property increases in value, you can take advantage of a home equity line of credit. This means that with the newly appraised value of your home and what you have put into the mortgage can be used to set up a line of credit. This line of credit can be used to invest in different opportunities. Finally, Liang claims that cash flow is another large benefit of investing in a property. Your property must bring in a positive net income in order for it to be profitable. Before purchasing a property, you must figure out how much you can rent it for and what your expenses are going to be. If it proves to not be profitable you must know to walk away and find the next property that will make you more money.

paragraphs.body: Investing in real estate is something that I want to do. Investing in real estate is something that I believe will be worth my time, money, and energy. My sister and I became interested in rental properties at separate times in our lives. It was only recently that I noticed that this was an interest that we both shared when I came across a photo that she had posted on social media of a book she had been reading about rental property investing. When I realized this was something that we were both interested in I immediately called her and we talked about our goals and plans for hours. As taking on a new interest like this can be expensive, we discussed teaming up and going through the journey together.

annotations.text: she had been reading about rental property investing. When I realized this was something that we were both interested in I immediately called her and we talked about our goals and plans for hours. As
annotations.comment: I'm interested and would like to know more about this! Let's talk.

paragraphs.body: My first real experience with real estate began when my dad mentioned that a friend of his named Joe was looking to sell a rental property he owned at Indiana University of Pennsylvania. As he was aware this was something my sister and I had recently been interested in, he gave me Joe’s number and I immediately called him to inquire about the place. We then set up a date to meet at his property and learn more about this experience. Liang states, “Even though the numbers are beautiful and crisp and you’re seeing the dollar signs, you might go in and realize this is not it.” In other words, Liang believes that sometimes you just need to see what you are getting into and touring the property is an essential step in finding a property that is right for you. I found myself making scheduling a tour for this place a top priority just like Liang, as I really wanted to make sure the property was worth the investment in its structure and physical qualities. After choosing a tour date, my sister, her boyfriend who is more well versed in assessing a property, and I were on our way to our first real estate experience. Joe was a very friendly older gentleman and taught us a lot about owning real estate on a college campus. It felt like he was trying to teach us rather than sell us on the place, which was very comforting. He showed us all around and explained his income and expenses on the place. Joe explained to us that you might not make much money on the first place that you own or the second, but when you start getting into your fourth and fifth and sixth you see the money add up. He had no other job for the past many decades and only managed college rentals. This was the lifestyle that I wanted to achieve.

annotations.text: This was the lifestyle that I wanted to achieve.
annotations.comment: why? Let's explore. Remember that you're not simply writing a report, or informational piece, about real estate investing. We need to figure out a meaningful, purposeful "central focusing idea" (with a clear audience and purpose) to create an essay.

paragraphs.body: According to Liang, “Income minus expenses folks, what’s coming in the door, what’s going out the door? If that number is positive, then you’re good to go.” Liang’s point is that in order to know if the property will really be worth investing in, you need to be profiting more than you are spending on it. After spending several hours with Joe and further discussion between my sister and I, we realized that this important point that Liang made did not seem to be the case for us. We figured out how much we could rent the place out for and we quickly noticed that our expenses would outweigh our profit. As we looked further into the property, we felt a few other factors that were pushing us further away from it. Another issue with this property was how far away it would be for both my sister and me. If something urgent came up, it would be very difficult for either of us to get down there in a timely manner to take care of the issue. One of the other deciding factors for us was the overall condition that the place was in. We noticed things such as cracks in the ceiling, windows that were in dire need of being replaced, and exposed wires in the basement. These were all things that could potentially lead to major expenses in the near future for us. With all these things in mind, we had to face the reality that this was not a rental property that would be a good first investment for us regardless of how excited we were for this opportunity.

annotations.text: With all these things in mind, we had to face the reality that this was not a rental property that would be a good first investment for us regardless of how excited we were for this opportunity.

paragraphs.body: My experience in investing in real estate is something I only see continuing to grow from here. Many priorities of Liang will also hold to be priorities of mine as my sister and I continue our search for a rental property. My experiences thus far have helped me to gain knowledge on the innerworkings of real estate, which I believe will prove to be very beneficial in finding a place that is a good fit for us and our interests.
